rationale: QuEra Aquila is Analog Hamiltonian Simulation (AHS) only — paradigm-incompatible with gate-model AND/XOR/MAJ/COPY logic circuits
Cross-substrate substrate-invariance comparison would require encoding boolean logic via Rydberg blockade graph problems (e.g., Maximum Independent Set isomorphic to MAJ on 4-vertex graph)
Honest framing: such an encoding tests COMPUTATIONAL EQUIVALENCE not GATE EQUIVALENCE — Φ proxy comparison would mix paradigms and weaken the substrate-invariance claim
Time budget: AHS encoding design + validation ≥30min; pilot already achieved primary substrate-invariance witness via SV1 ↔ IonQ Forte 1 comparison
Cost: would have been ~$1.30 (100 shots × $0.01 + $0.30 task) — affordable but low scientific marginal value at this stage
future_n12_extension: If substrate-invariance hypothesis warrants a paradigm-crossing test, design a Rydberg AHS protocol whose ground-state distribution computes a known boolean function (e.g., SAT instance) and compare its measurement Φ proxy against gate-model implementation of the same function. This is its own n_substrate_n13 or similar tracking item.
